records.ety: [F. rumeur, L. rumor; cf. rumificare, rumitare to rumor, Skr. ru to cry.]

defs: A flying or popular report; the common talk; hence, public fame; notoriety.
quotes: This rumor of him went forth throughout all Judea, and throughout all the region round about.
Great is the rumor of this dreadful knight.

defs: A current story passing from one person to another, without any known authority for its truth; -- in this sense often personified.
quotes: Rumor next, and Chance, And Tumult, and Confusion, all embroiled.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Rumored (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Rumoring.]

defs: To report by rumor; to tell.
quotes: 'T was rumored My father 'scaped from out the citadel.
